content.summary: Uses TouchDesigner's parameter callback system to execute Python code whenever custom parameters change. By defining an onValueChange callback in a parameter execute DAT or extension class, you can react to slider movements, button presses, menu selections, and other parameter edits in real time. This is the standard event-driven pattern for building interactive UI components and control panels.

codeSnippets.label: Parameter Execute DAT callbacks
codeSnippets.description: Standard callback structure for reacting to parameter value changes and pulse button presses.

codeSnippets.label: Extension-based parameter callback
codeSnippets.description: Extension method for handling parameter changes within the class, keeping logic co-located with state.

tips: Use par.eval() not par.val in callbacks to get the evaluated value when expressions are involved.
The prev argument in onValueChange gives you the previous value — useful for undo or delta calculations.
For Pulse parameters, use onPulse() not onValueChange() — pulse parameters do not trigger value changes.
Set the Parameter Execute DAT's 'Parameters' field to filter which parameters trigger callbacks, reducing unnecessary calls.
